ideas.headline: The only defence idea where studioFit clears 4. Bounded public corpus (Law 3978/2011, EU 2009/81, GDDIA opinions) means no classified data, no on-prem cleared deployment, and a recruitable co-founder (Greek defence-procurement lawyer, identifiable pool of ~30-50). UK MoD Defence Digital and US Tradewinds AI Marketplace precedents confirm the pattern is live in 2025. GDDIA is publicly publishing te
blockers: Recruit a Greek defence-procurement lawyer co-founder with 5+ years GDDIA or Karatzas/Zepos-equivalent experience.
Pilot scoped against ONE in-flight tender with quantified time-to-publication target (50% reduction).
Avoid touching classified annexes in v1 — public-corpus only — to keep deployment light and clearance unnecessary for v1.
Apply to HCDI 2nd Innovation Challenge as a soft-landing path.

ideas.headline: The most operationally compelling idea but the worst studio-fit. Precedent freshness is overwhelming: Anduril Lattice signed a $20B US Army enterprise agreement in March 2026, Helsing is closing a $1.2B round at $18B valuation, Tekever has £270M+ of UK MoD orders, Palantir signed a £240M UK MoD enterprise agreement Dec 2025. The category is unambiguously real. But the founder bar is brutal: an ex-
blockers: Cleared ex-Hellenic Navy O-5+ (KEPIH or J2/J3) signs founder paper before any T-Life cheque.
Defence-VC co-investor (NATO Innovation Fund, EDF-NATO DIANA partner, Helsing-pattern) LOIs minimum EUR 1.5M of the seed.
First wedge is OSINT-only (Greek + Turkish open-source) — explicitly NOT classified sensor fusion in v1 — to defer the clearance gate by 12 months.
Apply to HCDI Innovation Challenge unmanned-systems / C4I call as a co-funded proof point.

ideas.headline: Second-best studio-fit after the procurement clause-checker. No classified data ingest (TED, NSPA RFIs, GCC portals, defence press are all public/licensed-commercial), pilot budget EUR 180-400k is in-band, and the single buyer (General Secretariat for Defence Industry) shortens the sales cycle. EAS restructuring as a political backdrop is real but unstable — the GS could change with a minister res
blockers: Land ex-MFA defence-attaché or ex-EAS export-desk officer as commercial co-founder.
Get a written letter of interest from GS Defence Industry before the seed closes.
Pilot scoped to 90-day produce-50-opportunities milestone with attaché-comparable hit-rate measurement.
Pre-buy Janes / Forecast International data licences cost into the seed model (~EUR 80-150k/yr).

ideas.headline: Holds together structurally but is dragged down by lock-in risk: deferment fairness is a third-rail topic — any flagged unfairness becomes a press story, and the next defence minister can cancel the contract overnight to politically distance. Buyer is GEETHA Personnel (Directorate B1) plus Stratologia directorate — reachable but requires an ex-officer intermediary; not warm to T-Life today. Sensit
blockers: Position strictly as decision-support — humans approve every non-routine deferment.
Recruit ex-Stratologia officer co-founder AND an Athens-based data-protection counsel as advisor before signing any pilot.
Build a published fairness-metrics dashboard from day one as institutional armour against the press-cycle risk.
Pilot scoped to a single Stratologia branch (e.g. Athens or Thessaloniki) for a single conscription cycle.

ideas.headline: Source kill stands and the hostile pass confirms it. The political-third-rail finding (any flag on a politically-connected vendor terminates the relationship) is structural and uncovered by any redesign T-Life can fund. Lock-in risk is the worst in the portfolio: every government change and every minister reshuffle changes the protected-vendor list, and the operator becomes the messenger to be sho
